perturbation.original_text:
6. PAYMENT TERMS.
6.1 Payments. The parties shall make the payments described in Exhibit A. Overdue payments shall accrue interest, at the lesser of 1 1/2% per month or the maximum allowable interest under applicable law, from due date until paid, and the owing party shall pay the owed party's costs of collection (including reasonable attorneys' fees).
perturbation.changed_text:
6. PAYMENT TERMS.
6.1 Payments. The parties shall make the payments described in Exhibit A. Overdue payments shall not accrue interest, and the owing party shall not pay the owed party's costs of collection.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by stating that overdue payments will not accrue interest and the owing party will not pay collection costs. This directly contradicts the original provision in Section 6.1, which stipulated that overdue payments would accrue interest at 1 1/2% per month (or the maximum allowable rate) and that the owing party would be responsible for collection costs. This creates uncertainty about the financial penalties for late payments, potentially leading to disputes. The conflict emerges because Exhibit A defines specific payment amounts and schedules, creating an expectation that late payments are subject to the penalties described in the original version of Section 6.1. If penalties don't occur it will change how the exhibit A will apply to the co-branding agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.1 Payments. The parties shall make the payments described in Exhibit A. Overdue payments shall not accrue interest, and the owing party shall not pay the owed party's costs of collection.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 6.1 directly contradicts the original provision regarding interest accrual and collection costs for overdue payments, creating an inconsistency about financial penalties. The original text states: 'Overdue payments shall accrue interest, at the lesser of 1 1/2% per month or the maximum allowable interest under applicable law, from due date until paid, and the owing party shall pay the owed party's costs of collection'. Whereas the Changed Text states: 'Overdue payments shall not accrue interest, and the owing party shall not pay the owed party's costs of collection.' This is a clear and direct conflict.

perturbation.original_text:
8. TERM AND TERMINATION.
8.1 Term. This Agreement will become effective on the Effective Date and will continue in effect for 2 years following the Launch Date.
perturbation.changed_text:
8. TERM AND TERMINATION.
8.1 Term. This Agreement will become effective on the Effective Date and will continue in effect for 1 year following the Launch Date, unless extended by mutual agreement for an additional year.
perturbation.explanation: The term length has changed from 2 years to 1 year, with a possible extension for another year, This directly conflicts and introduces uncertainty about the duration of the agreement. This change affects Section 8.6 (Effects of Termination) where licenses granted will terminate upon expiration or termination of the current term agreement
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.6 Effects of Termination. Upon expiration or termination, all licenses granted hereunder shall terminate unless such licenses are expressly stated as surviving. Boxlot shall promptly remove all theglobe Marks and Page Templates from its servers, and theglobe shall promptly remove all Boxlot Marks, Boxlot Banners and Boxlot Content from its servers. Sections 1, 4.4 [Ownership], 5.1 [Rights/Restrictions], 6.2 [Taxes], 6.3 [Audit Rights], 8.6 [Effects of Termination], 9, 10, 11 and 12.2 [Governing Law] to 12.9 [Entire Agreement; Waiver], and any obligation to pay any owed but unpaid amounts, shall survive any expiration or termination. 7 In addition, Boxlot acknowledges that the placement fees set forth in Exhibit A were intended to be *** in year 1 and *** in year 2, even though theglobe has agreed to extend credit terms to Boxlot by allowing Boxlot to pay over time. Thus, in the event that this Agreement terminates under Section 8.2 [Termination for Failure to Perform] due to Boxlot's failure to perform (excluding the cross-reference to Section 12.7 [Force Majeure]) prior to the end of the applicable year, Boxlot shall immediately pay to theglobe the amount necessary to pay the total year's placement fee for that year. Boxlot waives any right to assert that the foregoing fee is a liquidated damage or a penalty.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the term length from 2 years to 1 year with an optional extension, which directly contradicts the contradicted text, particularly the section about placement fees intended for year 1 and year 2. The original text established a fixed two-year period, allowing for a reasonable expectation of these fees. The change creates uncertainty on how these fees would be applied if the agreement terminates after one year.

perturbation.original_text: 12.10 Equity Stake. Boxlot shall grant to theglobe, pursuant to industry- standard terms negotiated between the parties in good faith, immediately vested options to purchase shares of common stock equal to up to 4% of Boxlot's fully diluted capital stock (in accordance with the chart below). Theglobe shall be restricted from exercising such options until the first anniversary of the Effective Date, and such options shall have a five-year term.
perturbation.changed_text: 12.10 Equity Stake. Boxlot shall grant to theglobe, pursuant to industry- standard terms negotiated between the parties in good faith, immediately vested options to purchase shares of common stock equal to up to 2% of Boxlot's fully diluted capital stock (in accordance with the chart below). Theglobe shall be restricted from exercising such options until the second anniversary of the Effective Date, and such options shall have a three-year term.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation involves changing the equity stake granted to theglobe, reducing it from 4% to 2%. This contradicts the table that specifies percentage based on aggregate unique users under the domain name. This introduces legal uncertainty around the option quantity and exercise terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12.10 Equity Stake. Boxlot shall grant to theglobe, pursuant to industry- standard terms negotiated between the parties in good faith, immediately vested options to purchase shares of common stock equal to up to 2% of Boxlot's fully diluted capital stock (in accordance with the chart below). Theglobe shall be restricted from exercising such options until the second anniversary of the Effective Date, and such options shall have a three-year term. Each such option shall have an exercise price equal to the lesser of (1) the price at which Boxlot's common stock is sold to the public in an initial public offering or (2) $6 per share; each of the foregoing as may be adjusted for any stock splits, combinations or re-organizations affecting Boxlot's capital stock. Boxlot shall grant to theglobe registration rights for such options and any shares of common stock issued or issuable upon the exercise of such options (including without limitation, two demand registration rights and unlimited piggyback registration rights) on Form S-1, Form S-3 or such other form as may be applicable pursuant to the Securities Act of 1933 as amended. - ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ Source: THEGLOBE COM INC, S-1/A, 5/3/1999 Number of aggregate unique users, as measured by Aggregate percent of Boxlot's equity subject to the unique IP addresses, in a month who visit a page options upon achieving such level once under the Domain Name - ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ 500,000 1% - ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ 1,000,000 2% - ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ 1,500,000 3% - ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ 1,750,000 3.5% - ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ 2,000,000 4% - ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
perturbation.justification: The changed text reduces the equity stake from 4% to 2%, and alters the restriction and term period. The contradicted text contains a table which specifies equity stake percentages based on aggregate unique users, including a 4% stake for 2,000,000 users. By changing the stake percentage and other terms, a contradiction is introduced around what the actual equity stake is, creating a direct conflict with the explicit percentages specified in the table. This inconsistency will cause confusion regarding the amount of equity that theglobe is entitled to, leading to a dispute in contractual obligations and uncertainty and is therefore a strong in-text contradiction.
